content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444


November 8, 1982


Kirk Alan Pessner, Administrator
Political Reports/Research Unit
Dodds & Nielsen
Suite 2500, The Alcoa Bldg.
One Maritime Plaza
San Francisco, CA  94111

                                          Re:  Request for Advice on 2 Cal.
                                               Adm. Code Section 18419
                                               Our No. A-82-177

Dear Mr. Pessner:

     Thank you for your letter requesting advice on the
application of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18419(b)(1). Stated
succinctly, your question is as follows:

          Assuming that all other requirements of Section
     18419(b) are met, how close must the name of the PAC be
     to the name of the sponsoring organization in order to
     comply with 18419(b)(1) in light of the fact that the
     full name of the sponsoring organization must be
     included on the PAC's campaign statement (Form 420)?

     The specific example which you postulate is that of a
PAC called "San Joaquin Farmers for Good Government,"
sponsored by the "San Joaquin Valley Farm Association."
You ask whether this would be sufficient.

     On balance, we believe that insertion of the word
"Valley" in the name of the PAC, so that it would read
"San Joaquin Valley Farmers for Good Government" would
suffice to comply with the requirement of 18419(b).
Otherwise, confusion could result as to whether only
San Joaquin County farmers were involved.

     You have also asked a follow-up question:  whether a
sponsoring organization, which meets all of the criteria of
18419(b), must also file a Form 461 report?

Kirk Alan Pessner, Administrator
November 8, 1982
Page Two


If the sponsoring organization does not make a suffi-
cient amount of contributions and expenditures, other than
those in support of its PAC, to qualify as a committee
pursuant to Government Code Section 82013, and so long as
all the other criteria set out in Section 18419(b) are met,
the sponsoring organization has no obligation to file a
campaign disclosure statement. IF, however, the sponsoring
organization is required to file campaign disclosure state-
ments because all of the criteria in Section 18419(b) are
not met, it must report all of its campaign activity,
including its contributions and expenditures on behalf of
its PAC.

I trust that this answers your questions. However,
should you have any further questions regarding this
advice, please do not hesitate to call. You may reach me
at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division

REL:pln

DOBBS & NIELSEN
Attorneys and Counselors at Law

LOS ANGELES OFFICE
SUITE 309, 612 SOUTH FLOWER STREET
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90017
(213) 627-0300

A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

SUITE 2500, THE ALCOA BUILDING
ONE MARITIME PLAZA
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94111

Oct 23 1 17TH-BL
SACRAMENTO OFFICE

SUITE 850, 1225 EIGHTH STREET
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 446-6752

(415) 362-1940

ORANGE COUNTY OFFICE
SUITE 400, 4000 MACARTHUR BOULEVARD
NEWPORT BEACH, CALIFORNIA 92660
(714) 752-9316

October 21, 1982


Robert Stern, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Advice

Dear Mr. Stern:

     This letter requests advice regarding
2 Cal.Admin.Code 18419, "Organization Sponsoring Political
Action Committees."  Specifically, we request advice on
Section (b)(1) of Regulation 18419 that requires that the
name of the PAC include the official or publicly recognized
name of the sponsoring organization. We question whether
this name can be an adaptation of the sponsor's name when
clear identification still exists and because FPPC Form 420
now requires the sponsor's name on the form.

     Take for example a sponsoring organization that
complies with Section (b)(2) of Regulation 18419 by not
making sufficient other contributions than those to support
the PAC which would trigger a separate filing responsibility;
complies with Section (b)(3) whereby the PAC reports all con-
tributions made by the sponsoring organization complies with
(b)(4) by showing the sponsoring organization as an inter-
mediary on contributions required under the law; and by com-
plying with (b)(5) by having a responsible officer of the
sponsor also sign the report.

     The PAC chooses a name that contains the generic term
describing the general trade in which contributions are engaged.
The name of the PAC could be San Joaquin Farmers for Good
Government, but the name of the sponsoring organization is the
San Joaquin Valley Farm Association. Although the name of the
PAC is different from the sponsor, the Statement of Organiza-
tion shows the full name of the sponsor and the full name of
the sponsor is included on the campaign statement (FPPC Form
420), under the box at the top of the first page entitled
"Sponsoring Organization."

Robert Stern, Esq.
October 21, 1982
Page 2

          Would such a situation cause the sponsoring organization to be in compliance with 18419(b)(1) and therefore not required to file a separate Form 461? Thank you for your prompt response.

                              Kind regards,

                              Kirk Alan Pessner
                              Administrator
                              Political/Reports/
                              Research/Unit

KAP:tjd

sections.question: Assuming that all other requirements of Section 18419(b) are met, how close must the name of the PAC be to the name of the sponsoring organization in order to comply with 18419(b)(1) in light of the fact that the full name of the sponsoring organization must be included on the PAC's campaign statement (Form 420)? The specific example which you postulate is that of a PAC called "San Joaquin Farmers for Good Government," sponsored by the "San Joaquin Valley Farm Association." You ask whether this would be sufficient. You have also asked a follow-up question: whether a sponsoring organization, which meets all of the criteria of 18419(b), must also file a Form 461 report?
sections.conclusion: On balance, we believe that insertion of the word "Valley" in the name of the PAC, so that it would read "San Joaquin Valley Farmers for Good Government" would suffice to comply with the requirement of 18419(b). Otherwise, confusion could result as to whether only San Joaquin County farmers were involved. If the sponsoring organization does not make a sufficient amount of contributions and expenditures, other than those in support of its PAC, to qualify as a committee pursuant to Government Code Section 82013, and so long as all the other criteria set out in Section 18419(b) are met, the sponsoring organization has no obligation to file a campaign disclosure statement. If, however, the sponsoring organization is required to file campaign disclosure statements because all of the criteria in Section 18419(b) are not met, it must report all of its campaign activity, including its contributions and expenditures on behalf of its PAC.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a PAC name must closely match its sponsoring organization's name when the full sponsor name appears on campaign statements, and whether a sponsoring organization meeting Section 18419(b) criteria must file a Form 461 report.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The PAC name should include the word "Valley" to read "San Joaquin Valley Farmers for Good Government" to comply with Section 18419(b)(1) and avoid confusion. A sponsoring organization meeting all Section 18419(b) criteria with insufficient independent contributions need not file Form 461, but must do so if any Section 18419(b) criteria are unmet.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request (dated October 21, 1982) and FPPC response (dated November 8, 1982). Extracted response content from FPPC counsel letter. Both question and conclusion sections clearly present in FPPC response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Assuming that all other requirements of Section 18419(b) are met, how close must the name of the PAC be to the name of the sponsoring organization in order to comply with 18419(b)(1) in light of the fact that the full name of the sponsoring organization must be included on the PAC's campaign statement (Form 420)? The specific example which you postulate is that of a PAC called "San Joaquin Farmers for Good Government," sponsored by the "San Joaquin Valley Farm Association." You ask whether this would be sufficient. You have also asked a follow-up question: whether a sponsoring organization, which meets all of the criteria of 18419(b), must also file a Form 461 report?

CONCLUSION: On balance, we believe that insertion of the word "Valley" in the name of the PAC, so that it would read "San Joaquin Valley Farmers for Good Government" would suffice to comply with the requirement of 18419(b). Otherwise, confusion could result as to whether only San Joaquin County farmers were involved. If the sponsoring organization does not make a sufficient amount of contributions and expenditures, other than those in support of its PAC, to qualify as a committee pursuant to Government Code Section 82013, and so long as all the other criteria set out in Section 18419(b) are met, the sponsoring organization has no obligation to file a campaign disclosure statement. If, however, the sponsoring organization is required to file campaign disclosure statements because all of the criteria in Section 18419(b) are not met, it must report all of its campaign activity, including its contributions and expenditures on behalf of its PAC.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to inquiry regarding PAC naming requirements under Section 18419(b), advising that including the sponsor organization's geographic qualifier in the PAC name suffices for compliance. The Commission also clarifies that sponsoring organizations meeting all Section 18419(b) criteria are not required to separately file Form 461 unless they engage in sufficient independent campaign activity.
